pages.text: “Come, Jabulani,” Ma says. “Why aren’t you ready? You’ll be late for school.” “I can’t find my shoe,” says Jabulani.

blocks.text: “Come, Jabulani,” Ma says. “Why aren’t you ready? You’ll be late for school.”
“I can’t find my shoe,” says Jabulani.

pages.text: “Did you look in the wardrobe?” Ma asks. “Yes, it’s not in the wardrobe.” “Did you look under your bed?” says Ma. “It’s not under my bed,” Jabulani says.

blocks.text: “Did you look in the wardrobe?” Ma asks. “Yes, it’s not in the wardrobe.”
“Did you look under your bed?” says Ma. “It’s not under my bed,” Jabulani says.

pages.text: “I know where your shoe is,” giggles Jabulani’s sister Lungi. “Where?” Jabulani asks. “I’m not telling you!” says Lungi “Somebody’s taken it,” Jabulani says.

blocks.text: “I know where your shoe is,” giggles Jabulani’s sister Lungi. “Where?” Jabulani asks.
“I’m not telling you!” says Lungi
“Somebody’s taken it,” Jabulani says.

pages.text: “Why would someone take one shoe when they’ve got two feet?” says Lungi. “I don’t know. Maybe they’ve hidden it to make me cross.”

blocks.text: “Why would someone take one shoe when they’ve got two feet?” says Lungi.
“I don’t know. Maybe they’ve hidden it to make me cross.”

pages.text: “Did you take my shoe?” he asks Lungi. “No,” says Lungi, “your shoes are too big for me.”

blocks.text: “Did you take my shoe?” he asks Lungi.
“No,” says Lungi, “your shoes are too big for me.”

pages.text: “Did you take my shoe?” Jabulani asks Fruit Juice the Dog. She smiles her big dog smile at him and wags her tail.

blocks.text: “Did you take my shoe?” Jabulani asks Fruit Juice the Dog.
She smiles her big dog smile at him and wags her tail.

pages.text: “Maybe a bird picked it up and flew away with your shoe,” laughs Lungi.

blocks.text: “Maybe a bird picked it up and flew away with your shoe,” laughs Lungi.

pages.text: “Maybe a monkey took it and left it up in a tree,” says Jabulani.

blocks.text: “Maybe a monkey took it and left it up in a tree,” says Jabulani.

pages.text: “Don’t worry, we’ll find your shoe,” says Ma. “Everyone look for Jabulani’s shoe.” But the shoe is nowhere to be found.

blocks.text: “Don’t worry, we’ll find your shoe,” says Ma. “Everyone look for Jabulani’s shoe.”
But the shoe is nowhere to be found.

pages.text: “It’s all right,” says Ma. “You can wear your takkies to school today.”

blocks.text: “It’s all right,” says Ma. “You can wear your takkies to school today.”

pages.text: Lungi comes rushing into the kitchen with a big smile on her face. “I know where your shoe is,” she shouts. “Come and see.”
